summary: Backend testing for distributions router migration completed. All 18 tests passed. The distributions endpoints have been successfully migrated from server.py to routers/distributions.py. All write operations use require_write_access dependency for subscription gating, and read operations use get_current_user.
passed_tests: GET /api/benevolence-log - returns benevolence aggregates with correct structure (200)
POST /api/distributions - creates new distribution with active subscription (200)
POST /api/distributions with is_benevolence=true - creates benevolence distribution (200)
POST /api/distributions with is_benevolence=true missing required fields - returns 400
critical_code_review_comments: Router correctly imports and uses require_write_access for all write operations (POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE)
Read operations (GET /distributions, GET /benevolence-log) correctly use get_current_user (allows read-only access)
MongoDB _id is excluded using {'_id': 0} projection in all queries
Benevolence validation properly requires recipient_name and need_description when is_benevolence=true
Email notifications are sent via background_tasks for async processing
Distribution approval requires both solvency_confirmed and recusal_acknowledged
Router is properly included in server.py at line 7025 with prefix '/api'
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_ prefixed distributions during testing for CRUD verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Distributions router fully tested. All CRUD operations working. Write operations (POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE) use require_write_access dependency which blocks 403 when subscription is expired. Read operations (GET) use get_current_user allowing access regardless of subscription status. PurposeClassification enum values are: distribution, compensation, expense, other (not discretionary/benevolent).
